PROJECTS.title: Toughbook AutoInstaller
PROJECTS.subtitle: CLI Automation Toolkit — Bash & Python
PROJECTS.description: Command-line toolkit that automates hardware detection, driver installation, and OEM preparation for Panasonic Toughbook refurbishment.
PROJECTS.detailDescription:
Overview

Toughbook AutoInstaller is a modular command-line toolkit built for technicians who refurbish Panasonic Toughbook rugged laptops and tablets. It runs on Ubuntu LTS and provides an interactive menu that automates the entire QA workflow — from first boot to OEM-ready state.

1 / 3

The Problem

In a refurbishment environment, each unit must be individually tested to ensure all components — 4G modem, GPS, fingerprint reader, touch screen, cameras, smart card reader — are functional before shipping. Doing this manually on every unit takes 30+ minutes per device and is highly error-prone. Different technicians follow different steps, leading to inconsistent configurations and missed defects.

The Solution

A single script that detects the Toughbook model automatically and presents a tailored interactive menu. The toolkit handles hardware scanning, driver installation, audio configuration, touch calibration, and full OEM preparation — all from one terminal session.

Why It Matters

This project was born from a real bottleneck at Rugged Books. Every day, dozens of units needed identical configuration, and the manual process was the single biggest constraint on throughput. By automating detection, driver installation, and system preparation, the toolkit cut per-unit setup time by over 60% and eliminated configuration drift between technicians.

Impact on Productivity

  • 60%+ reduction in per-unit configuration time
  • Zero configuration drift — every unit ships with identical setup regardless of which technician handled it
  • Faster onboarding — new hires follow the script instead of memorizing a 30-step manual process
  • Scalable — adding support for new models requires only a new menu definition and detection rules
  • Fewer returns — automated hardware verification catches defects before shipping

Supported Models

  • CF-31 MK5 — Ubuntu 22.04
  • CF-53 MK4 — Ubuntu 22.04
  • CF-54 MK2 — Ubuntu 22.04
  • CF-C2 MK2 — Ubuntu 22.04
  • FZ-G1 MK1 / MK4 — Ubuntu 20.04

Architecture

The project is organized into modular Bash scripts under a lib/ directory. Each module handles a specific responsibility:

toughbook-autoinstall/
├── autoinstall.sh              # Entry point
├── lib/
│   ├── colors.sh               # Terminal color variables
│   ├── utils.sh                # Spinner, banner, checks
│   ├── sysinfo.sh              # System info (CPU, RAM, battery)
│   ├── detection.sh            # Hardware detection
│   ├── drivers.sh              # Driver installation
│   ├── tools.sh                # Keyboard test, audio, touch
│   ├── oem.sh                  # OEM preparation (Sysprep)
│   └── menus.sh                # Interactive menus
├── alsamixerconf.sh            # ALSA audio config
├── c2audioconf.sh              # CF-C2 audio config
├── touch-calibrator.sh         # Touch calibration (CF-53)
├── touch-calibrator-cf31.sh    # Touch calibration (CF-31)
└── README.md

Documentation — How to Use

Prerequisites

  • A Panasonic Toughbook from the supported models list
  • Ubuntu LTS installed (20.04 or 22.04 depending on model)
  • Internet connection (Wi-Fi or Ethernet)
  • Root access

Quick Start

cd ~/Downloads
sudo apt install git -y
git clone https://github.com/AndresVGu/toughbook-autoinstall
cd toughbook-autoinstall
chmod +x autoinstall.sh
sudo ./autoinstall.sh

Menu Options

  1. Device Information — Displays system specs (CPU, RAM, storage, battery)
  2. Hardware Detection — Scans for connected peripherals
  3. Update Device — Installs updates and drivers for detected hardware
  4. Test Keyboard — Launches a keyboard testing utility
  5. Sound Activation — Configures ALSA audio (model-specific)

Device Information

Option 1 scans and displays full system specifications including CPU model, RAM capacity, storage drives, and battery health — giving the technician a quick overview of the unit's hardware.

Hardware Detection

Option 2 performs a deep scan of all connected peripherals — USB devices, serial ports, Bluetooth, cameras, GPS modules, fingerprint readers, and smart card readers. Each component is flagged as detected or not detected.

Test Keyboard

Option 4 launches a visual keyboard testing utility that highlights each key as it's pressed, ensuring all keys are functional before shipping.

OEM Preparation

After all tests pass, the script runs oem-config-prepare to reset the system for end-user delivery. The unit shuts down ready to be shipped or cloned.

Future Potential

The modular architecture means the toolkit can expand to support new hardware models, integrate with inventory management systems, generate automated QA reports, and potentially feed data into a dashboard for production line monitoring — turning a simple script into a full refurbishment operations platform.

PROJECTS.title: NBD Landscaping
PROJECTS.subtitle: Business app with ASP.NET MVC and C#
PROJECTS.description: Role-based web app for a landscaping business — manage clients, assign projects, and generate bids with material and labor costs.
PROJECTS.detailDescription: NBD Landscaping is a full-featured business management application built for a landscaping company. The system implements role-based authentication so each employee only sees and manages what they're authorized to. Managers can oversee all clients and projects, while field employees handle their assigned work. The bid system calculates total project costs by combining material quantities and labor hours, giving the business accurate estimates before committing to a job.

PROJECTS.title: Cities API
PROJECTS.subtitle: ASP.NET Core Web API
PROJECTS.description: REST API that maps cities to their provinces, with full CRUD support on every entity.
PROJECTS.detailDescription: Cities API is a RESTful web service built with ASP.NET Core that exposes endpoints for querying cities and their corresponding provinces. It supports full CRUD operations on both entities, follows REST conventions, and includes proper HTTP status codes and error handling. The project demonstrates clean API design patterns and serves as a practical example of building data-driven services with .NET.

PROJECTS.title: Spotify Clone
PROJECTS.subtitle: Built with Astro
PROJECTS.description: Functional Spotify clone — play songs and browse albums with a clean, responsive UI.
PROJECTS.detailDescription: A Spotify-inspired music player built with Astro and vanilla JavaScript. Users can browse a curated library of albums, play tracks with a persistent bottom player, and navigate between different album views. The project focuses on smooth UI interactions, audio state management across page navigations, and replicating the feel of a real streaming app without a backend.

PROJECTS.title: Emma's Small Engines
PROJECTS.subtitle: Business app with vanilla HTML5, CSS3 & JavaScript
PROJECTS.description: Inventory and repair management system for a small engine shop — track equipment, orders, and service records.
PROJECTS.detailDescription: Emma's Small Engines is a business management tool built entirely with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript — no frameworks. It handles the day-to-day operations of a small engine repair shop: tracking inventory levels, logging equipment details, managing repair orders, and keeping service history. The project demonstrates that clean, functional business software doesn't always require a heavy stack.

PROJECTS.title: VitGreen
PROJECTS.subtitle: eCommerce Website
PROJECTS.description: Modern eCommerce store for protein products — secure checkout, product reviews, and fast shipping.
PROJECTS.detailDescription: VitGreen is a modern eCommerce platform specializing in protein and health products. The site features a clean product catalog with detailed descriptions, a customer review system, and a streamlined checkout flow with secure payment integration. The design prioritizes conversion — clear CTAs, fast load times, and a mobile-first layout that makes browsing and buying easy on any device.

PROJECTS.title: V12 Magazine
PROJECTS.subtitle: Car Magazine
PROJECTS.description: Automotive magazine site covering news and events from the Colombian car scene.
PROJECTS.detailDescription: V12 Magazine is a digital automotive publication covering the Colombian car culture — from local racing events and car shows to industry news and vehicle reviews. The site was built to handle a content-heavy layout with fast load times, a clean reading experience, and easy navigation between categories. It served as a real publication used by the Colombian automotive community.
